title: Content JSON schemas (allow-list/entry/translation/review) plus CI structural check
context: oncology-glossary-multilingual builds an open, multilingual, plain-language glossary of oncology and clinical-trial terms. Structured content schemas and CI validation are essential to enforce safety-critical fields (notMedicalAdvice, sourceRefs, reviewer sign-offs) consistently. The schema lives in the agent-neutral core (packages/schema) per Elyos architecture rules; no vendor/agent-specific logic belongs in adapters.
objective: Add termSourceAllowListSchema, glossaryEntrySchema, glossaryTranslationSchema, and reviewSchema to packages/schema/src/schemas.ts, compiled via validate.ts (AJV + ajv-formats), and wire a structural-check script into pnpm test so CI fails on any non-conformant content file.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/schema/src/schemas.ts includes termSourceAllowListSchema, glossaryEntrySchema, glossaryTranslationSchema, and reviewSchema compiled with AJV and ajv-formats

A structural-check script is wired into pnpm test and fails CI if: notMedicalAdvice is not true, sourceRefs is empty, a delivered or done entry lacks required sign-offs, or a YAML content file is malformed or non-conformant
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass on the PR
All schemas live in packages/schema/src/schemas.ts (agent-neutral core; no vendor/agent-specific logic in adapters/)
YAML artifacts in sources/ and glossary/ are parsed to JSON and validated by the structural-check script
resources: PLAN.md — data model section for artifact structures
output: PR adding content schemas to packages/schema/src/schemas.ts and a structural-check CI script wired into pnpm test
